name: Denver International Airport (DEN)
content:
I suspect the rumors about Denver airport to be true. The place feels like Ikea. When I first visited, I had to go through a construction area to get to my gate (which is fine, construction happens). This involved descending down a bare-concrete stairwell for seemingly several stories, walking through a bare-concrete chamber well illuminated by overhead fluorescent lights, located some unknown distance underground, before re-ascending another set of endless concrete stairs leading to an area of pure white drywall and towering unfinished ceilings with exposed mechanical spaces. I travelled through this region with a crowd of other travellers, all moving in unison, all illuminated in whites and greys, all unspeaking.
The remainder of the airport is a pleasant experience. My notes say that all the hot people hang out around gates B61 through B71. I am not sure whether this is still accurate. The Dunkin Donuts does not serve Chai Lattes.
